roles.title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Play's apps ecosystem — managing the app discovery, distribution, and developer experience on Android's primary app marketplace.
roles.why_good_fit: Google Play is a massive consumer platform with deep India relevance — India is Android's largest market. Jay's experience scaling Resso on Android (Tier 2/3 India users, ML-driven discovery) maps to app discovery challenges. His understanding of Indian consumer segments (regional users, GenZ, informal businesses) is valuable for Play's India growth. His ByteDance experience with app ecosystem dynamics (TikTok, Resso) gives him platform-level thinking. 5 years PM experience required matches his ~7 years.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Resso experience as app ecosystem expertise — you built and scaled an app from alpha to India's #1 in its category on Android. You understand app discovery, user acquisition, and retention in the Indian market. Highlight your insight about Tier 2/3 users being underserved by global products — this applies directly to Google Play's India opportunity. Mention your competitive intelligence work (TikTok re-entry strategy) to show you understand platform dynamics. Emphasize your AI fluency as relevant to AI-powered app discovery.
roles.job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Play Apps — the world's largest app marketplace. Day-to-day involves defining features that improve app discovery, developer tools, or user engagement, working with engineering teams, analyzing marketplace data, and driving growth metrics. Success means growing app installs, improving developer satisfaction, and enhancing the user experience of finding and using apps.

roles.title: Senior Product Manager, Android Developer Console
roles.role_summary: Senior PM for Android Developer Console — the tool developers use to publish, manage, and grow their apps on Google Play.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has 5+ years PM experience (requirement is 5 years) and 2+ years of zero-to-one product building (Resso founding team, travel startup). His experience with app ecosystems (Resso on Android, TikTok) gives him developer-side perspective. His understanding of Indian developers and SMBs could be valuable for Developer Console's India market. However, this is a developer tools/B2B product, not a consumer product — it's a reasonable but not ideal fit. His AI fluency could be relevant for AI-powered developer tools.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Resso experience from the developer/publisher perspective — you understand what app developers need because you've been one. Highlight your understanding of the Indian developer ecosystem. Emphasize your zero-to-one experience and your ability to build tools that serve diverse user segments (Tier 2/3 developers, not just Silicon Valley). Mention your AI OS as evidence you can envision AI-powered developer tools.
roles.job_description: You'll own product areas within Android Developer Console — the primary tool developers use to publish apps, manage releases, analyze performance, and grow their apps on Google Play. Day-to-day involves defining developer-facing features, working with engineering teams, gathering developer feedback, and shipping tools that improve the developer experience. Success means developers finding it easier and more effective to build and grow apps on Android.

roles.title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
roles.role_summary: PM for payments risk — managing fraud detection, risk assessment, and trust & safety for Google's payment products.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's SMB over-enforcement work at Meta is an almost perfect analogy — he identified that Meta's global enforcement model was creating false positives that devastated Indian SMBs, then drove policy change and a new agentic resolution system. This is fundamentally a payments risk problem: balancing fraud prevention with legitimate user access. He understands the India-specific nuances of risk (informal businesses, different CRM patterns, voice-led operations) that global risk models miss. The 3-year minimum experience requirement means Jay comes in as a very senior candidate with 7 years.
roles.how_to_win: This is your SMB enforcement story on steroids. Lead with: 'At Meta, I discovered that our global enforcement model was over-blocking legitimate Indian SMBs — I drove the creation of a new product team, an agentic resolution system, and delivered a 2.3% India revenue uplift.' Frame this as a payments risk problem: false positives in enforcement = false positives in fraud detection. Emphasize that you understand the India-specific risk landscape — informal businesses that look suspicious to global models but are perfectly legitimate. Show you can balance risk mitigation with user growth.
roles.job_description: You'll own the risk management product surface for Google's payment products — defining how fraud is detected, how legitimate transactions are protected, and how risk models are tuned. Day-to-day involves working with ML teams on risk models, analyzing fraud patterns, defining policy frameworks, and ensuring the right balance between security and user experience. Success means reducing fraud losses while minimizing false positives that block legitimate users.

roles.title: Product Manager, Cloud Database
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Cloud's database products — managing database services for enterprise Cloud customers.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay meets the 5-year PM experience requirement and has zero-to-one building experience. However, this role requires 2 years of database/analytics/big data product experience, which Jay lacks. It's a deeply technical B2B infrastructure role without India market growth or AI-native components. His consumer product and India market strengths don't translate strongly here.
roles.how_to_win: This is a stretch role. If applying, emphasize your technical product intuition and ability to learn domains quickly. Frame your data analysis work at Meta (Unidash, opportunity sizing) as adjacent. Highlight your AI-assisted data analysis capabilities. However, this is likely not the best use of Jay's application energy.
roles.job_description: You'll own Google Cloud database products — managed database services used by enterprise customers for their applications. Day-to-day involves defining database product roadmaps, working with database engineering teams, gathering enterprise customer requirements, and shipping features. Success means growing database service adoption and customer satisfaction.

roles.title: AI Deployment Engineer, Startups
roles.role_summary: AI Deployment Engineer role at OpenAI focused on startups, based remotely in India. Part of the Technical Success team — likely involves helping startups integrate and deploy OpenAI's products. Limited job description available.
roles.why_good_fit: OpenAI is Jay's top target company. Remote India is his P0 preference. The startup ecosystem focus aligns with his entrepreneurial background and understanding of Indian SMBs/startups. His top-percentile AI tool fluency and personal AI operating system demonstrate deep product-level understanding of AI deployment. However, the 'Engineer' title suggests a technical implementation role which may not be ideal.
roles.how_to_win: Worth investigating the full JD — the 'Technical Success' team framing may be more consultative/GTM than pure engineering. If so, Jay should lead with his AI fluency (top 5% usage at current company, personal AI OS, multi-agent workflow experience) and startup founder background. Highlight ability to understand startup needs from first principles and translate AI capabilities into business outcomes. Flag risk: if this is a pure coding/engineering role, it's a discard.
roles.job_description: AI Deployment Engineer, Startups. Technical Success team. India - Remote. Limited details available.

roles.title: AI Deployment Manager - India
roles.role_summary: Manages AI deployment and technical success for OpenAI's enterprise/startup customers in India — ensuring successful adoption and integration of OpenAI's APIs and products.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has relevant experience working with SMBs and enterprises on AI adoption (MetaAI India field work covering enterprises, SMBs, and AI enthusiasts). His understanding of how Indian businesses actually operate (voice-led, informal digital literacy) is valuable for deployment strategy. His AI fluency is exceptional (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS with multiple agents). The India-remote setup is ideal. However, this is a Technical Success role — likely more customer-facing and implementation-focused than Jay's core product/growth skillset. His experience is more market intelligence and product strategy than deployment management.
roles.how_to_win: Lean into your understanding of how Indian businesses adopt technology — the SMB enforcement story shows you understand the gap between how global products assume Indian businesses work vs. reality. Highlight your AI OS as proof you can deeply understand deployment challenges. Position your field work with enterprises and SMBs during MetaAI India as direct experience understanding AI adoption barriers. Be transparent that your strength is the market intelligence and user understanding layer, and frame that as the missing piece in deployment success — technical deployment fails when you don't understand the user context.
roles.job_description: This role manages the deployment and adoption of OpenAI's AI products for customers in India. Day-to-day likely involves working with Indian enterprises and startups to successfully integrate OpenAI's APIs, troubleshooting adoption challenges, and ensuring customers achieve value from the products. Success looks like high customer satisfaction, successful deployments, and growing usage.
